name: Smoky Tempeh Buddha Bowl

ingredients.item: small sweet potato, chopped into bite-size pieces

method: Place the sweet potatoes on a baking sheet. Drizzle the oil over the top and lightly toss. Sprinkle with salt. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until they can be easily pierced with a fork. Set aside.

Meanwhile, cook the quinoa. Place quinoa in a sieve and rinse well. In a small saucepan, combine quinoa and broth. Bring to a boil, cover, and reduce to a simmer. Cook for 10 to 15 minutes or until the broth is absorbed. Remove from the heat and let set with the cover on for 5 minutes.
Cut the tempeh into ¼-inch slices and then cube.
Mix all the ingredients together in a small bowl until smooth and well combined.

Fill each salad bowl with the spinach. Make a decorative rim with the spinach tips if so desired. Divide the quinoa and vegetables between the two bowls and lay in a circle: peppers, quinoa, cabbage, sweet potatoes, and lastly, tempeh. Drizzle the dressing in a circle over all.
